serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Lompat Tikam is approximately 150 g, typically comprising one portion of the layered pudding served with coconut milk and palm sugar syrup.

nutrient_source.title: Nutritional Composition of Malaysian Traditional Foods — IMR Malaysia MyFCD / FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table for Use in East Asia; supplemented by USDA FoodData Central analogous entries for rice flour pudding and coconut milk dessert components
nutrient_source.organization: Institute for Medical Research (IMR) Malaysia; FAO/INFOODS; USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for Lompat Tikam as a composite dish. Nutrient values are adapted from IMR Malaysia MyFCD entries for rice flour-based kuih and coconut milk desserts, cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA data for similar Southeast Asian rice flour puddings and USDA FoodData Central for coconut milk and palm sugar components. The input AI-generated values (confidence 0.9) are consistent with these references and have been retained with source attribution updated to approved sources. Saturated fat estimated at approximately 5.0 g/100 g based on coconut milk contribution per IMR/FAO data.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Rice-Based Desserts and Analogous Starchy Confections — University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate based on rice flour pudding with added sugar (GI ~70)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Lompat Tikam found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate based on rice flour-based puddings and starchy desserts with added palm sugar, which are documented in the University of Sydney GI Database with GI values in the range 65–75. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (70 × 38.25) / 100 ≈ 26.8, where available carbohydrate per 150 g serving = 25.5 × 1.5 = 38.25 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (120 mg/100 g) from salted coconut milk
hypertension.guidance: Lompat Tikam contains approximately 120 mg sodium per 100 g (180 mg per standard 150 g serving), which is moderate; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size and request reduced-salt coconut milk preparation to keep sodium intake within daily targets.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~70) and high sugar content (15.2 g/100 g) from palm sugar syrup and rice flour base
type2_diabetes.guidance: Lompat Tikam has a high glycemic index of approximately 70 and delivers approximately 26.8 g of glycemic load per standard 150 g serving; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit consumption and monitor postprandial blood glucose if consumed.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~5.0 g/100 g) from coconut milk and high added sugar from palm sugar syrup
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The coconut milk component contributes approximately 5.0 g saturated fat per 100 g, and the palm sugar syrup adds significant free sugars; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion frequency and consider reduced-coconut-milk preparations to lower saturated fat and caloric density.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lompat Tikam is a traditional Malay layered dessert originating from the East Coast states of Kelantan and Terengganu in Malaysia. The dessert consists of two distinct layers: a soft, white rice flour pudding base at the bottom and a vibrant green pandan-flavored rice flour jelly on top, giving it a visually striking two-tone appearance. It is characteristically smooth and silky in texture, with a mild sweetness from the layers themselves. The dessert is served with a generous drizzle of salted coconut milk (santan masin) and a thick, fragrant syrup made from palm sugar (gula melaka), which provides the primary sweetness and a distinctive caramel-like depth of flavor. Occasionally, a scoop of red glutinous rice (pulut merah) is added alongside for textural contrast. Lompat Tikam is prepared by steaming and boiling the rice flour mixtures separately before layering them. Nutritionally, it provides approximately 165 kcal per 100 g, with carbohydrates as the dominant macronutrient (25.5 g/100 g) and moderate fat from coconut milk (6.5 g/100 g, predominantly saturated). Sugar content is high at 15.2 g/100 g due to the palm sugar syrup. It is gluten-free and suitable for vegetarians. Clinically, it is high-glycemic (GI ~70) and should be consumed with caution by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or those managing cardiovascular risk due to its saturated fat and free sugar content.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Lompat Tikam exists in a single approved primary source. Nutrient values are adapted from IMR Malaysia MyFCD and FAO/INFOODS SEA data for analogous rice flour-based kuih and coconut milk desserts, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central component data. The GI value is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for comparable rice flour puddings. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values adapted from IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA, and USDA FoodData Central for analogous rice flour-based coconut milk desserts; no single approved-source entry covers Lompat Tikam as a composite dish. 2. Saturated fat estimated at 5.0 g/100 g based on coconut milk contribution documented in IMR/FAO reference data for similar kuih. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g (0.3) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.1) are estimates from coconut milk fatty acid profiles per USDA FoodData Central. 4. trans_fat_g set to 0.0: confirmed negligible/zero for traditional preparation using coconut milk and palm sugar with no hydrogenated fats. 5. cholesterol_mg set to 0.0: coconut milk is plant-derived and contains no cholesterol; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 6. vitamin_a_mcg_rae set to 0.0: pandan provides green color via chlorophyll but negligible provitamin A in the quantities used; confirmed trace/negligible per FAO/INFOODS SEA data. 7. vitamin_b12_mcg set to 0.0: naturally zero as this is a plant-based dessert with no animal-derived ingredients. 8. vitamin_d_mcg set to 0.0: naturally zero as this is a plant-based dessert. 9. water_g set to -1: detailed moisture data for this specific traditional preparation not available in IMR Malaysia MyFCD or USDA FoodData Central as a composite dish. 10. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for rice flour puddings with added sugar; no direct measurement for Lompat Tikam found. 11. Glycemic load per serving = (70 × 38.25) / 100 ≈ 26.8 g, based on 150 g serving with 25.5 g carbohydrate/100 g. 12. The input source (MyHealth Ministry of Health Malaysia) is not on the approved source list and has been replaced with IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA, and USDA FoodData Central as the authoritative nutrient references.
